grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: before and after
grammarPoints.rule: Use phrases with the words 'before' and 'after' to show the order of two events. (e.g. 'She feeds her fish before school.')
grammarPoints.pattern: verb phrase + before/after + noun phrase  or  Before/After + noun phrase, + clause

intro_examples.text: What does she do before breakfast? She gets dressed before breakfast.
intro_examples.jp: 彼女は朝食の前に何をしますか？ 彼女は朝食の前に服を着ます。

intro_examples.text: What does he do after school? He feeds his bird after school.
intro_examples.jp: 彼は放課後に何をしますか？ 彼は放課後に鳥にえさをあげます。

table.title: Showing Order of Events
notes: We use phrases with 'before' and 'after' to show when things happen.
If before or after is at the beginning of the sentence, we use a comma after the phrase: 'Before school, she feeds her fish.'

tab1_samples.text: He plays with his cat after school.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は放課後に猫と遊びます。

tab1_samples.text: I brush my teeth after breakfast.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は朝食の後に歯を磨きます。

tab1_samples.text: He washes his face before breakfast.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は朝食の前に顔を洗います。

tab1_samples.text: She feeds her hamster after school.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女は放課後にハムスターにえさをあげます。

tab1_samples.text: I make my bed before school.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は学校に行く前にベッドを整えます。

tab1_samples.text: I have a snack after school.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は放課後におやつを食べます。

tab1_samples.text: I do my homework before dinner.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は夕食の前に宿題をします。

tab1_samples.text: I take a shower after school.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は放課後にシャワーを浴びます。

tab1_samples.text: He plays basketball after school.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は放課後にバスケットボールをします。

tab1_samples.text: We hold hands before we cross the street.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは通りを渡る前に手を繋ぎます。

rules.title: Use before and after to show order of events
rules.jp_title: 出来事の順序を示すために before と after を使う
rules.subtitle: Use 'before' to show the first action happens first, and 'after' to show the second action happens first.
rules.jp_subtitle: 1番目の行動が最初に行われる場合は「before」を、2番目の行動が最初に行われる場合は「after」を使います。

transforms.from: I eat breakfast. I brush my teeth. (order)
transforms.to: I brush my teeth after breakfast.

examples.text: I brush my teeth after breakfast.
examples.jp: 私は朝食の後に歯を磨きます。

examples.text: He washes his face before breakfast.
examples.jp: 彼は朝食の前に顔を洗います。

rules.title: Place the phrase at the beginning with a comma
rules.jp_title: 文頭に置くときはカンマを使う
rules.subtitle: When a before/after phrase is placed at the beginning of the sentence, separate it with a comma.
rules.jp_subtitle: before や after の句を文頭に置くときは、カンマで区切ります。

transforms.from: She feeds her fish before school.
transforms.to: Before school, she feeds her fish.

examples.text: Before school, she feeds her fish.
examples.jp: 学校に行く前に、彼女は魚にえさをあげます。

examples.text: After breakfast, I brush my teeth.
examples.jp: 朝食の後に、私は歯を磨きます。

rules.title: Using full clauses with before and after
rules.jp_title: before や after の後ろに節（主語＋動詞）を置く
rules.subtitle: You can follow before and after with either a simple noun or a full subject and verb clause.
rules.jp_subtitle: before や after の後ろには、名詞だけでなく、主語と動詞からなる節を置くこともできます。

transforms.from: I always take care of my dog before school.
transforms.to: I always take care of my dog before I go to school.

examples.text: I always take care of my dog before I go to school.
examples.jp: 学校に行く前に、私はいつも犬の世話をします。

examples.text: I play with Fluff after he eats.
examples.jp: フラフがご飯を食べた後に、私は彼と遊びます。

mixed_samples.text: Before school, she feeds her fish.
mixed_samples.jp: 学校に行く前に、彼女は魚にえさをあげます。

mixed_samples.text: After breakfast, I brush my teeth.
mixed_samples.jp: 朝食の後に、私は歯を磨きます。

mixed_samples.text: I always take care of my dog before I go to school.
mixed_samples.jp: 学校に行く前に、私はいつも犬の世話をします。

mixed_samples.text: I play with Fluff after he eats.
mixed_samples.jp: フラフがご飯を食べた後に、私は彼と遊びます。

mixed_samples.text: After school, I take Fluff for a walk.
mixed_samples.jp: 放課後に、私はフラフを散歩に連れて行きます。

mixed_samples.text: I do my homework before dinner.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は夕食の前に宿題をします。

mixed_samples.text: I watch TV after school.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は放課後にテレビを見ます。

mixed_samples.text: I feed my hamster before dinner.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は夕食の前にハムスターにえさをあげます。

mixed_samples.text: I read a book before bedtime.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は寝る前に本を読みます。

mixed_samples.text: We eat dinner after my father comes home.
mixed_samples.jp: お父さんが家に帰ってきた後に、私たちは夕食を食べます。

stem: school. (He does it when school finishes.)

explanation.title: Showing Order of Events
explanation.body: Since he does it when school finishes, 'after' is the correct preposition: 'after school'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は放課後に（学校が終わった後に）猫と遊びます。

stem: breakfast. (She does it before she eats.)

explanation.title: Showing Order of Events
explanation.body: To show she does it first before eating, use 'before': 'before breakfast'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼女は朝食の前に歯を磨きます。

explanation.title: Grammar Structure
explanation.body: In a question with 'does', we use the base form of the verb 'do'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は放課後に何をしますか？

explanation.title: Third Person Singular
explanation.body: For 'she', we use the auxiliary verb 'does' to make questions in the simple present.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼女は朝食の前に何をしますか？

stem: school. (He does it in the morning before going.)

explanation.title: Showing Order of Events
explanation.body: He feeds it in the morning before going, so the order is 'before school'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は学校に行く前に鳥にえさをあげます。

stem: school, she feeds her fish. (Don't forget the comma!)

explanation.title: Time Phrase Punctuation
explanation.body: 'Before school' can be placed at the beginning of the sentence, followed by a comma.

tab3_quiz.jp: 学校に行く前に、彼女は魚にえさをあげます。

explanation.title: Showing Order of Events
explanation.body: Since the action is done after eating, 'after' is correct.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私は朝食の後に歯を磨きます。

explanation.title: Healthy Habits
explanation.body: We wash hands first to stay clean, so we use 'before': 'before we eat'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私たちは夕食を食べる前に手を洗うべきです。

stem: school. (I do it after returning home.)

explanation.title: Showing Order of Events
explanation.body: Since you return from school first, you do homework 'after school'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私は放課後に宿題をします。

explanation.title: Bedtime Routine
explanation.body: To ask about preparations before going to sleep, 'before' is used: 'before bedtime'.

tab3_quiz.jp: あなたは寝る前に何をしますか？

stem: Choose the correct sentence with proper capitalization and punctuation.
answers: Before school, she feeds her fish.
Before school she feeds her fish.
She feeds her fish before school,.
Before school, she feeds her fish,.

explanation.title: Punctuation Rules
explanation.body: When starting a sentence with a 'before' or 'after' phrase, a comma is required right after that phrase.

tab4_master.jp: 大文字とカンマ、ピリオドが正しく使われている文を選んでください。

stem: What is the first event in the timeline: 'I brush my teeth after breakfast'?

explanation.title: Understanding Order
explanation.body: 'After breakfast' means eating breakfast happens first, and brushing teeth happens second.

tab4_master.jp: 「I brush my teeth after breakfast」という文で、最初に行われることはどれですか？

stem: What is the second event in the timeline: 'She feeds her fish before school'?

explanation.title: Understanding Order
explanation.body: 'Before school' means feeding her fish happens first, and going to school happens second.

tab4_master.jp: 「She feeds her fish before school」という文で、2番目に行われることはどれですか？

stem: Which of the following sentences has completely correct punctuation?
answers: After breakfast, I brush my teeth.
After breakfast I brush my teeth.
After breakfast, I brush my teeth,.
After breakfast I brush, my teeth.

explanation.title: Punctuation Rules
explanation.body: A comma is placed after introductory phrases ('After breakfast') but not before or after other words.

tab4_master.jp: カンマが正しく使われている文を選んでください。

stem: Choose the correct answer for the question:

A: 'What does she do before school?'
B: 'She ________ her hamster.'

explanation.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
explanation.body: 'She' is third person singular, so we use 'feeds'.

tab4_master.jp: 質問に対する正しい答えを選んでください。

tab4_master.cue: Build: He plays with his cat after school.
tab4_master.jp: 彼は放課後に猫と遊びます。

tab4_master.cue: Build: I brush my teeth after breakfast.
tab4_master.jp: 私は朝食の後に歯を磨きます。

tab4_master.cue: Build: Before school, she feeds her fish.
tab4_master.jp: 学校に行く前に、彼女は魚にえさをあげます。

tab4_master.cue: Build: After breakfast, I brush my teeth.
tab4_master.jp: 朝食の後に、私は歯を磨きます。

tab4_master.cue: Build: We hold hands before we cross the street.
tab4_master.jp: 私たちは通りを渡る前に手を繋ぎます。

japanese.title: before と after
japanese.rule: 「before」（～の前）と「after」（～の後）の語句を使って、2つの出来事の順序を表します。語句が文の最初に来るときはカンマ（,）で区切ります。
japanese.pattern: 動詞句 + before/after + 名詞句  または  Before/After + 名詞句, + 節

examples.sentence: He plays with his cat after school.
I brush my teeth after breakfast.
He washes his face before breakfast.
She feeds her hamster after school.
I make my bed before school.
I have a snack after school.
I do my homework before dinner.
I take a shower after school.
He plays basketball after school.
We hold hands before we cross the street.
Before school, she feeds her fish.
After breakfast, I brush my teeth.
I always take care of my dog before I go to school.
I play with Fluff after he eats.
After school, I take Fluff for a walk.
I watch TV after school.
I feed my hamster before dinner.
I read a book before bedtime.
We eat dinner after my father comes home.

source.note: Workbook page 7 and Grammar Workbook pages 2-3 used.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Adverbs of frequency
grammarPoints.rule: Adverbs of frequency describe how often we do something. They typically come before the main verb in a sentence (with verbs other than 'be').
grammarPoints.pattern: subject + adverb of frequency + verb phrase

intro_examples.text: I never eat lunch at 12:30. (0% of the time)
intro_examples.jp: 私は絶対に（never）12:30に昼食を食べません。

intro_examples.text: I sometimes eat lunch at 12:30. (Some of the time)
intro_examples.jp: 私は時々（sometimes）12:30に昼食を食べます。

intro_examples.text: I usually eat lunch at 12:30. (Most of the time)
intro_examples.jp: 私は普通（usually）12:30に昼食を食べます。

intro_examples.text: I always eat lunch at 12:30. (100% of the time)
intro_examples.jp: 私はいつも（always）12:30に昼食を食べます。

table.title: Adverbs of Frequency Chart
notes: always = every time; at all times
usually = not at all times, but most times (normal routine)
sometimes = on some occasions (not a normal routine)
never = not ever; not one time
The adverb of frequency goes BEFORE the main verb.

tab1_samples.text: I always feed my pet in the morning.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はいつも朝にペットにえさをあげます。

tab1_samples.text: He usually eats a snack after school.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼はたいてい放課後におやつを食べます。

tab1_samples.text: We sometimes play with our dog at the park.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは時々公園で犬と遊びます。

tab1_samples.text: They never go to bed late.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼らは絶対に遅く寝ることはありません。

tab1_samples.text: Omar always gets up early.
tab1_samples.jp: オマールはいつも早く起きます。

tab1_samples.text: He usually eats breakfast at six o'clock.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼はたいてい6時に朝食を食べます。

tab1_samples.text: He sometimes eats breakfast at six thirty.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は時々6時半に朝食を食べます。

tab1_samples.text: He never makes his bed.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は絶対にベッドを整えません。

tab1_samples.text: I usually make my bed in the morning.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はたいてい朝にベッドを整えます。

tab1_samples.text: I always make my bed in the morning.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はいつも朝にベッドを整えます。

rules.title: Position of the Adverb of Frequency
rules.jp_title: 頻度を表す副詞の位置
rules.subtitle: The adverb typically goes before the main verb in a sentence, but after the form of the verb 'be'.
rules.jp_subtitle: 一般動詞の文では動詞の前に、be動詞の文ではbe動詞の後ろに副詞を置きます。

transforms.from: I brush always my teeth before bed.
transforms.to: I always brush my teeth before bed.

examples.text: I always brush my teeth before bed.
examples.jp: 私は寝る前にいつも歯を磨きます。

examples.text: I usually help my grandmother after school.
examples.jp: 私はたいてい放課後におばあちゃんを手伝います。

rules.title: Use usually for routines
rules.jp_title: 通常のルーティンには usually を使う
rules.subtitle: Use 'usually' to describe things you normally do as part of your day-to-day routine.
rules.jp_subtitle: 日頃のルーティンとして普段行っていることには「usually」を使います。

transforms.from: I sometimes walk to school because I do it four days a week.

examples.jp: 私たちは時々歩いて学校に行きます。
彼らは絶対に部屋を掃除しません。

mixed_samples.text: I always brush my teeth before bed.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は寝る前にいつも歯を磨きます。

mixed_samples.text: I usually help my grandmother after school.
mixed_samples.jp: 私はたいてい放課後におばあちゃんを手伝います。

mixed_samples.text: We sometimes walk to school.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは時々歩いて学校に行きます。

mixed_samples.text: They never clean their rooms.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼らは絶対に部屋を掃除しません。

mixed_samples.text: Tamara always rides her bike.
mixed_samples.jp: タマラはいつも自転車に乗ります。

mixed_samples.text: Kiko sometimes takes the dog to the park.
mixed_samples.jp: キコは時々犬を公園に連れて行きます。

mixed_samples.text: Olga never does her homework before school.
mixed_samples.jp: オルガは学校の前に絶対に宿題をしません。

mixed_samples.text: Marco usually helps at home.
mixed_samples.jp: マルコはたいてい家でお手伝いをします。

mixed_samples.text: She usually has a snack at three forty-five.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼女はたいてい3時45分におやつを食べます。

mixed_samples.text: He never comes home at eight o'clock.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼は絶対に8時に帰宅しません。

stem: gets up early. (100% of the time)

explanation.title: Adverbs of Frequency
explanation.body: Since it happens 100% of the time, the correct adverb is 'always'.

tab3_quiz.jp: オマールはいつも（100%）早く起きます。

stem: eats breakfast at 6:00. (Most of the time)

explanation.title: Adverbs of Frequency
explanation.body: For actions that happen most of the time but not every time, 'usually' is correct.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は普通・たいてい（Most of the time）6時に朝食を食べます。

explanation.title: Adverbs of Frequency
explanation.body: For actions that happen only on some occasions, we use 'sometimes'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は時々（some times）6時半に朝食を食べます。

stem: makes his bed. (0% of the time)

explanation.title: Adverbs of Frequency
explanation.body: For an action that happens 0% of the time, 'never' is the correct adverb.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は絶対に（0%）ベッドを整えません。

explanation.title: Simple Present Tense
explanation.body: With 'I' as the subject, the simple present verb form 'make' is correct.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はたいてい朝にベッドを整えます。

explanation.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
explanation.body: 'Tamara' is third person singular (she), so the verb needs an -s: 'rides'.

tab3_quiz.jp: タマラはいつも自転車に乗ります。

explanation.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
explanation.body: 'Kiko' is third person singular, so we use 'takes'.

tab3_quiz.jp: キコは時々犬を公園に連れて行きます。

explanation.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
explanation.body: 'Olga' is third-person singular, so the auxiliary verb/action verb is 'does'.

tab3_quiz.jp: オルガは学校の前に絶対に宿題をしません。

explanation.title: Adverb Position
explanation.body: The adverb 'always' goes directly before the action verb 'play': 'I always play soccer.'

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はいつもサッカーをします。

explanation.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
explanation.body: 'Marco' is third person singular (he), so the correct verb form is 'helps'.

tab3_quiz.jp: マルコはたいてい家でお手伝いをします。

stem: Which adverb of frequency means 'not ever or not one time'?

explanation.title: Adverbs of Frequency Definitions
explanation.body: 'Never' means 0% of the time, or not at any time.

tab4_master.jp: 「一度もない、絶対に～ない」を意味する頻度の副詞はどれですか？

stem: Which adverb of frequency means 'every time or at all times'?

explanation.title: Adverbs of Frequency Definitions
explanation.body: 'Always' means 100% of the time, or at all times.

tab4_master.jp: 「いつも、常に」を意味する頻度の副詞はどれですか？

stem: Which of the following sentences has the adverb of frequency in the correct position?
answers: I always brush my teeth before bed.
I brush always my teeth before bed.
Always I brush my teeth before bed.
I brush my teeth before bed always.

explanation.title: Word Order
explanation.body: Adverbs of frequency must be placed directly before the main action verb ('always brush').

tab4_master.jp: 頻度の副詞の位置が正しい文を選んでください。

stem: Choose the grammatically correct sentence from the options.
answers: She usually has a snack after school.
She has usually a snack after school.
Usually she has a snack after school.
She has a snack after school usually.

explanation.title: Word Order
explanation.body: The adverb 'usually' must go before the main verb 'has': 'usually has'.

tab4_master.jp: 文法的に正しい文を選んでください。

stem: Complete the dialogue with the most appropriate response:

A: 'How often do you make your bed?'
B: 'I ________ make my bed in the morning.'

explanation.title: Responding to How Often
explanation.body: Questions asking 'how often' should be answered with an adverb of frequency like 'usually'.

tab4_master.jp: 対話を完成させるのに最も適切な言葉を選んでください。

tab4_master.cue: Build: Omar always gets up early.
tab4_master.jp: オマールはいつも早く起きます。

tab4_master.cue: Build: He usually eats breakfast at six o'clock.
tab4_master.jp: 彼はたいてい6時に朝食を食べます。

tab4_master.cue: Build: He never makes his bed.
tab4_master.jp: 彼は絶対にベッドを整えません。

tab4_master.cue: Build: Tamara always rides her bike.
tab4_master.jp: タマラはいつも自転車に乗ります。

tab4_master.cue: Build: Olga never does her homework before school.
tab4_master.jp: オルガは学校の前に絶対に宿題をしません。

japanese.title: 頻度の副詞
japanese.rule: 物事を行う頻度を表す言葉（always, usually, sometimes, never）を使います。これらの副詞は、一般動詞の前、be動詞の後ろに置きます。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + 頻度の副詞 + 動詞句

examples.sentence: I always feed my pet in the morning.
He usually eats a snack after school.
We sometimes play with our dog at the park.
They never go to bed late.
Omar always gets up early.
He usually eats breakfast at six o'clock.
He sometimes eats breakfast at six thirty.
He never makes his bed.
I usually make my bed in the morning.
I always make my bed in the morning.
I always brush my teeth before bed.
I usually help my grandmother after school.
We sometimes walk to school.
They never clean their rooms.
Tamara always rides her bike.
Kiko sometimes takes the dog to the park.
Olga never does her homework before school.
Marco usually helps at home.
She usually has a snack at three forty-five.
He never comes home at eight o'clock.

source.note: Workbook page 10 and Grammar Workbook pages 4-5 used.
